description: Synthesis, decomposition, single replacement, double replacement, and combustion reactions, including predicting products and balancing equations.

problems.problem: List three pieces of evidence that a chemical reaction has occurred.

step_by_step.explanation: Chemical reactions often produce visible changes.

step_by_step.calculation: Color change, gas production (bubbles), temperature change (heat or cold), precipitate formation, light emission, odor change.

problems.final_answer: Possible evidence: color change, gas production, temperature change, precipitate formation, light emission.

problems.problem: Classify and balance the reaction: H₂ + O₂ → H₂O

step_by_step.explanation: Two elements combining to form a compound → synthesis reaction.

step_by_step.calculation: Reactants: 2 H, 2 O; Products: 2 H, 1 O
Place coefficient 2 before H₂O: H₂ + O₂ → 2H₂O

problems.problem: Classify and balance the reaction: H₂O → H₂ + O₂

step_by_step.explanation: One compound breaking down into elements → decomposition reaction.

step_by_step.calculation: Reactants: 2 H, 1 O; Products: 2 H, 2 O
Place coefficient 2 before H₂O: 2H₂O → H₂ + O₂

problems.problem: Classify and balance the reaction: Zn + HCl → ZnCl₂ + H₂

step_by_step.explanation: One element replaces another in a compound → single displacement.

step_by_step.calculation: Reactants: 1 Zn, 1 H, 1 Cl; Products: 1 Zn, 2 H, 2 Cl
Place coefficient 2 before HCl: Zn + 2HCl → ZnCl₂ + H₂

problems.problem: Classify and balance the reaction: AgNO₃ + NaCl → AgCl + NaNO₃

step_by_step.explanation: Two compounds exchange ions → double displacement (precipitation).

step_by_step.calculation: Reactants: 1 Ag, 1 NO₃, 1 Na, 1 Cl; Products: 1 Ag, 1 Cl, 1 Na, 1 NO₃

step_by_step.explanation: Already balanced.

problems.final_answer: Double displacement: AgNO₃ + NaCl → AgCl + NaNO₃

problems.problem: Classify and balance the reaction: CH₄ + O₂ → CO₂ + H₂O

step_by_step.explanation: Hydrocarbon reacting with oxygen → combustion reaction.

step_by_step.calculation: 4 H on left, 2 H on right → place coefficient 2 before H₂O: CH₄ + O₂ → CO₂ + 2H₂O
O on right: 2 + 2 = 4 O; on left: 2 O → place coefficient 2 before O₂: CH₄ + 2O₂ → CO₂ + 2H₂O

problems.problem: Balance the equation: Fe + O₂ → Fe₂O₃

step_by_step.calculation: 1 Fe on left, 2 Fe on right → place coefficient 2 before Fe: 2Fe + O₂ → Fe₂O₃
2 O on left, 3 O on right → need common multiple 6. Place coefficient 3 before O₂ (6 O) and 2 before Fe₂O₃ (6 O): 4Fe + 3O₂ → 2Fe₂O₃
4 Fe on left, 4 Fe on right, balanced.

problems.problem: Balance the equation: Al + CuSO₄ → Al₂(SO₄)₃ + Cu

step_by_step.calculation: 1 Al on left, 2 Al on right → place coefficient 2 before Al: 2Al + CuSO₄ → Al₂(SO₄)₃ + Cu
1 SO₄ on left, 3 SO₄ on right → place coefficient 3 before CuSO₄: 2Al + 3CuSO₄ → Al₂(SO₄)₃ + Cu
3 Cu on left, 1 Cu on right → place coefficient 3 before Cu: 2Al + 3CuSO₄ → Al₂(SO₄)₃ + 3Cu

problems.problem: Predict the products of the synthesis reaction: Ca + O₂ →

step_by_step.explanation: Synthesis: two elements combine to form a compound.
Calcium (Group 2) forms Ca²⁺; oxygen forms O²⁻ → calcium oxide, CaO.

problems.problem: Predict the products of the decomposition reaction: H₂CO₃ →

step_by_step.explanation: Decomposition: carbonic acid breaks down into water and carbon dioxide.

problems.problem: Predict the products of the single displacement reaction: Mg + AgNO₃ →

step_by_step.explanation: Magnesium is more reactive than silver, so Mg will replace Ag.

problems.problem: Predict the products of the single displacement reaction: Cu + AgNO₃ →

step_by_step.explanation: Copper is less reactive than silver? Wait, copper is above silver, so Cu will replace Ag.

problems.problem: Predict the products of the double displacement reaction: Pb(NO₃)₂ + KI →

step_by_step.explanation: AB + CD → AD + CB

problems.problem: Predict the products of the combustion reaction: C₃H₈ + O₂ →

step_by_step.explanation: Hydrocarbon + oxygen → carbon dioxide + water.

problems.problem: Write the net ionic equation for: AgNO₃(aq) + NaCl(aq) → AgCl(s) + NaNO₃(aq)

step_by_step.explanation: Dissociate soluble ionic compounds.
step_by_step.calculation: Ag⁺(aq) + NO₃⁻(aq) + Na⁺(aq) + Cl⁻(aq) → AgCl(s) + Na⁺(aq) + NO₃⁻(aq)

step_by_step.explanation: Na⁺ and NO₃⁻ appear on both sides.

problems.problem: Write the net ionic equation for: NaOH(aq) + HCl(aq) → NaCl(aq) + H₂O(l)

step_by_step.calculation: Na⁺(aq) + OH⁻(aq) + H⁺(aq) + Cl⁻(aq) → Na⁺(aq) + Cl⁻(aq) + H₂O(l)

step_by_step.explanation: Na⁺ and Cl⁻ appear on both sides.

problems.problem: Classify the following reaction: 2KClO₃ → 2KCl + 3O₂

step_by_step.explanation: One compound breaks down into simpler substances.

problems.problem: Classify the following reaction: 2Mg + O₂ → 2MgO

step_by_step.explanation: Two elements combine to form one compound.

problems.problem: Classify the following reaction: Fe + CuSO₄ → FeSO₄ + Cu

step_by_step.explanation: One element replaces another in a compound.

problems.problem: Classify the following reaction: Na₂SO₄ + BaCl₂ → BaSO₄ + 2NaCl

step_by_step.explanation: Two compounds exchange ions to form two new compounds.

problems.problem: Balance and classify: C₆H₁₂O₆ + O₂ → CO₂ + H₂O

step_by_step.explanation: Glucose reacting with oxygen → combustion.

step_by_step.calculation: 6 C on left → place 6 before CO₂: C₆H₁₂O₆ + O₂ → 6CO₂ + H₂O
12 H on left → place 6 before H₂O: C₆H₁₂O₆ + O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O
O on right: 12 + 6 = 18 O; O on left: 6 from glucose + 2 from O₂ → need 12 more O → 6 O₂: C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂ → 6CO₂ + 6H₂O

problems.problem: Predict whether a reaction will occur when zinc is placed in copper(II) sulfate solution. If yes, write the balanced equation.

step_by_step.explanation: Zinc is more reactive than copper, so Zn will replace Cu.

problems.final_answer: Yes, reaction occurs: Zn + CuSO₄ → ZnSO₄ + Cu

problems.problem: Predict whether a reaction will occur when copper is placed in zinc sulfate solution. If yes, write the balanced equation.

step_by_step.explanation: Copper is less reactive than zinc, so Cu cannot replace Zn.

problems.final_answer: No reaction (copper is below zinc in the activity series)

problems.problem: Write the balanced equation and classify: Calcium oxide reacts with water to produce calcium hydroxide.

step_by_step.explanation: CaO + H₂O → Ca(OH)₂

step_by_step.calculation: 1 Ca, 1 O + 1 O? Wait: CaO has 1 O, H₂O has 1 O → product has 2 O. Balanced.

step_by_step.explanation: Two compounds combining → synthesis.
